task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Menu Name' text input field contains the exact text 'Main Navigation'.

task_completion.description: 'Home' and 'Our Products' are added as Custom Links.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for menu item boxes labeled 'Home' and 'Our Products' in the menu structure area. They should have 'Custom Link' indicated on the right side of their boxes. If expanded, the URL for 'Home' should be 'http://localhost/' and for 'Our Products' should be '#'.

task_completion.description: 'About Us', 'Blog', and 'Contact' are added as standard pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for menu item boxes labeled 'About Us', 'Blog', and 'Contact' in the menu structure area. They should have 'Page' indicated on the right side of their boxes.

task_completion.description: 'Cakes' and 'Pastries' are added and nested under 'Our Products'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for menu item boxes labeled 'Cakes' and 'Pastries'. They must be positioned below 'Our Products' and visually indented to the right, indicating they are sub-items.

task_completion.description: The menu is assigned to the Primary menu location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Menu Settings' section at the bottom of the menu structure. The checkbox next to 'Primary menu' (or similar primary location depending on the theme) must be checked.
Look for a WordPress success banner at the top of the page (usually with a green left border) saying 'Menu has been updated.' or 'Main Navigation has been created.'.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress web interface to create the menu.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard (Appearance -> Menus) being interacted with in a web browser, not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual WordPress application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show the local WordPress URL (e.g., wp-admin/nav-menus.php) and the interface should be the genuine WordPress admin panel.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client or terminal to insert the menu items directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of MySQL prompts, phpMyAdmin, or terminal scripts being used to bypass the WordPress GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected menu name is 'Main Navigation'. The expected items are 'Home', 'About Us', 'Our Products', 'Cakes', 'Pastries', 'Blog', and 'Contact'. The expected theme location is 'primary'. The task operates on synthetically generated WordPress pages ('About Us', 'Cakes', 'Pastries', 'Blog', 'Contact').
